title: Catalonia Conquered: Alex Baker Runs Away (Then Blows Up, Then Runs Away Again)
title_candidates: Catalonia Conquered: Alex Baker Runs Away (Then Blows Up, Then Runs Away Again)
Twelve and Counting: A Procession in Girona
The Sextuple King: How Alex Baker Won a TEG He Kept Trying to Lose
theme: Alex Baker treated TEG 18 like a procession — front by twelve after R1 and never threatened — and yet seemingly couldn't get through a round without a number that began with a one. Around him, Gregg Williams quietly built a 14-shot Jacket from a R1 base that included two quadruples, and Jon Baker squandered an early Jacket lead so completely he ended up holding the Spoon.
opening_hook: Open on the scoreboard after R1: Alex Baker +46, the field a distant rumour. Then rewind to how, exactly, a man who would card an 11, a 10, an 8 and three nines still won by eight.
foreshadow: Alex Baker's R1 closing double on 17 (b41) and the 9 on 18 (b16) — the first hints that the runaway leader had a self-destruct button
Jon Baker briefly seizing the Jacket lead on R2 H9 (b46) — the high-water mark before the long slide to the Spoon
Gregg Williams's R1 horror show (b17, b18, b31) sitting oddly alongside a man who would lift the Green Jacket by 14

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: A wire-to-wire procession after the briefest of wobbles. Alex went outright on R1 H3 (b-arc), surrendered the lead for a single hole to David Mullin on H4, retook it on H5 and never gave it back. He banked the lead on R1 with rounds of 46 and a 17-point burst on holes 8–12 (b09), held a 12-shot cushion at halfway despite an 11 on the Tour 18th (b03), and put the result beyond doubt with 21 points in six holes on the Stadium back nine in R3 (b02). 169 points, by 8 from John Patterson — 7th best Trophy score in TEG history.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: A slow-burn that began in chaos. Gregg shipped two quadruples in R1 (b17, b18) and was 3rd at +27. R2 he steadied (a 13-point par run on 13–16, b26) and closed to 2nd. Jon Baker had led from the start and even held outright after R2 H9 (b46), but on R3 H2 — with a quiet par — Gregg moved into outright Jacket lead for the first time (b12) and never looked back. From there: rounds of 13 and 12 on the brutal Stadium course, while everyone else folded. Winning margin: 14, the kind of gap that looks like a typo.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Lost in plain sight. Jon ended R1 propping up the field, climbed off the bottom in R2 (a 39-point round, capped by 16 points in the closing five including a birdie at 14 — b14, b34) and even led the Jacket outright at one stage. Then the Stadium happened. A 9 at the 4th in R3 (b10) was the loudest noise, but the actual moment of doom was R3 H2 — a triple bogey that returned him to the bottom (b27), where he stayed. R3 28 pts, R4 29 pts, including a 10-shot bleed across holes 7–11 in R4 (b48). 126 points, last.

headline_candidates: 46 and Out of Sight: Alex Baker's Statement on the Tour
Carnage on the Tour: Five Men, Many Quadruples, One Runaway
rounds.chosen_headline: 46 and Out of Sight: Alex Baker's Statement on the Tour
rounds.angle: The Trophy race is effectively settled in 18 holes: Alex 46, everyone else more than 10 adrift — and that's despite a quadruple at the 2nd and a 9 on 18. Meanwhile the Jacket bunches around Jon Baker, Mullin and Williams, with Patterson's eight-hole, 22-shot bleed (b07) the day's worst meltdown.

headline_candidates: Jon Baker's Jacket — For One Hole
Tour Course, Take Two: Same Leader, Same Story
rounds.angle: Alex makes an 11 at the last (b03) and an 8 at the 16th (b08) and still leads the Trophy by 11. Jon Baker briefly grabs outright Jacket lead on H9 (b46) and closes with 16 points in five (b14). Behind it all, the Spoon position changes hands four times — Williams, Jon Baker (twice), Mullin — a sign of what's coming.

headline_candidates: Stadium Sorting: Williams Seizes, Jon Baker Sinks
One Quiet Par, One Decisive Triple
The Hole That Decided Two Trophies
rounds.chosen_headline: The Hole That Decided Two Trophies
rounds.angle: R3 H2 does the heavy lifting: a routine Williams par takes outright Jacket lead for the first time (b12); a Jon Baker triple drops him to the bottom of the Spoon for keeps (b27). Alex Baker's mid-round bleed (b43) barely dents the lead, and a 21-point run on 11–16 (b02) puts the Trophy beyond reach.

headline_candidates: Eight, Fourteen, and the Spoon Sealed
rounds.angle: Nothing to overturn: Alex closes out the Trophy (38 pts, even with a 10 at 14 — b04 — and an 8 at 17 — b19), Gregg adds another 12 to win the Jacket by 14, Jon Baker's 10-shot stretch on 7–11 (b48) confirms the Spoon.

players.arc: Champion as demolition site: led from R1 H5 to the final putt, never less than 11 clear at any round-end, and yet produced four separate scores of 8 or worse — an 11, a 10, two 8s and a 9 — and still won by eight.
From 3rd and bleeding in R1 (two quadruples, +27) to Green Jacket by 14 — a steady, almost stealthy ascent that took outright lead with a par on R3 H2 and was never headed.
Held the Jacket lead through R1 and R2, even briefly outright, then a triple at R3 H2 dropped him into the Spoon position he never escaped — Catalonia's most thorough fall.
Trophy runner-up despite shipping 22 shots in eight holes on the opening morning (b07); a back-nine specialist (b30, b13, b28, b33, b44) who quietly out-pointed everyone bar the runaway leader.
Jacket runner-up and brief Trophy leader on R1 H4 — but his week was punctuated by an 11, two more 8s, and a stretch of three quadruples in R2 (b49).

venue_notes: TEG's 4th visit to Catalonia, Spain — and a familiar Camiral double-bill: two laps of the Tour (5th and 6th TEG rounds at that venue) before the Stadium showdown (6th and 7th visits). Use Tour-as-warm-up / Stadium-as-judge framing: the Tour was where Alex banked the cushion and the Spoon shuffled; the Stadium, 'Spain's premier course', is where Gregg's Jacket move and Jon's Spoon were sealed (both on R3 H2).
